steps.title: Identify the connecting thought from the first sentence
steps.content: The first sentence introduces Rabinal Achí as a dance drama. The second sentence must continue this thought, explaining what the dance drama conveys or depicts. After the introductory phrase “Based on events that occurred when Rabinal was a city-state ruled by a king,” the blank should re-introduce Rabinal Achí (the subject from the previous sentence) and directly state what it does—namely, that it tells a story.

steps.title: Match the structural and logical flow to the choices
steps.content:
A directly bridges the two sentences: “Rabinal Achí tells the story of K'iche' Achí, a military leader who had once been an ally of the king but was later captured...” This maintains focus on the dance drama as the subject while introducing the character and his historical role within a relative clause.

Options B and C shift the main subject away from the dance drama to the character himself, breaking the connection to the previous sentence. Option D introduces an awkward existential structure (“there was”) and places the prepositional phrase “in Rabinal Achí“ between the relative pronoun “who” and the verb, violating standard relative clause word order.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
